input.description: Check loop test for migration pattern

data.output:
1	package config
2	
3	import "time"
4	
5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;
7	// there are no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
8	//
9	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors.
10	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
11		Assignee                string
12		ReviewMaxRetries        int
13		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
14		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
15		HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
16		Harness                 string
17		Model                   string
18		Profile                 string
19		MinTier                 string
20		MaxTier                 string
21		EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
22	}
23	
24	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
25	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose loop-relevant
26	// accessors return exactly the values supplied in opts.
27	//
28	// Tests must obtain ResolvedConfig via Resolve on the returned *Config
29	// — there is no shortcut to construct a ResolvedConfig directly. Pure
30	// CLI-override fields (Assignee, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are passed
31	// through CLIOverrides at the call site; the constructor stores every
32	// other field on *Config so it round-trips through Resolve.
33	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config {
34		reviewMaxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
35		maxNoChanges := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
36	
37		caps := opts.EvidenceCaps
38	
39		return &Config{
40			Version: "1.0",
41			Agent: &AgentConfig{
42				Harness: opts.Harness,
43				Model:   opts.Model,
44			},
45			Workers: &WorkersConfig{
46				NoProgressCooldown:      opts.NoProgressCooldown.String(),
47				MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: &maxNoChanges,
48				HeartbeatInterval:       opts.HeartbeatInterval.String(),
49			},
50			ReviewMaxRetries: &reviewMaxRetries,
51			EvidenceCaps:     &caps,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
56	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
57	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
58	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
59	// applied at Resolve time only.
60	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
61		return CLIOverrides{
62			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
63			Profile:  opts.Profile,
64			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
65			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
66		}
67	}
68

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"regexp"
9		"strings"
10		"sync"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
16	)
17	
18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
19	// Durable knobs (Assignee, ReviewMaxRetries, NoProgressCooldown,
20	// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose, HeartbeatInterval, Harness, Model, Provider,
21	// ModelRef, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are stripped — they live on
22	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's rcfg argument.
23	// Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation runtime intent remain.
24	//
25	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Bead 6.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log          io.Writer
28		EventSink    io.Writer
29		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
30		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
31		Once         bool
32		PollInterval time.Duration
33		NoReview     bool
34		LabelFilter  string
35		SessionID    string
36		WorkerID     string
37		ProjectRoot  string
38	}
39	
40	// RunWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to Run. It accepts a sealed
41	// ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and a ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime (plumbing
42	// + per-invocation intent) and delegates to Run under the hood. Behavior
43	// is identical to Run with an equivalently-populated ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
44	//
45	// Stage 1 of SD-024: this method exists alongside Run; production callers
46	// have not migrated yet.
47	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) RunWithConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
48		opts := ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
49			Assignee:                rcfg.Assignee(),
50			Once:                    runtime.Once,
51			PollInterval:            runtime.PollInterval,
52			NoProgressCooldown:      rcfg.NoProgressCooldown(),
53			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose(),
54			HeartbeatInterval:       rcfg.HeartbeatInterval(),
55			Log:                     runtime.Log,
56			NoReview:                runtime.NoReview,
57			ReviewMaxRetries:        rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries(),
58			EventSink:               runtime.EventSink,
59			ProgressCh:              runtime.ProgressCh,
60			PreClaimHook:            runtime.PreClaimHook,
61			WorkerID:                runtime.WorkerID,
62			ProjectRoot:             runtime.ProjectRoot,
63			Harness:                 rcfg.Harness(),
64			Model:                   rcfg.Model(),
65			Profile:                 rcfg.Profile(),
66			Provider:                rcfg.Provider(),
67			ModelRef:                rcfg.ModelRef(),
68			SessionID:               runtime.SessionID,
69			LabelFilter:             runtime.LabelFilter,
70			MinTier:                 rcfg.MinTier(),
71			MaxTier:                 rcfg.MaxTier(),
72		}
73		return w.Run(ctx, opts)
74	}
75	
76	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
77	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
78	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
79	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
80	
81	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
82		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
83		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
84		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
85		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
86		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
87		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
88		Status      string `json:"status"`
89		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
90		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
91		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
92		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
93		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
94		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
95		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
96		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
97		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
98		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
99		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
100		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
101		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
102		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
103		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
104		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
105		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
106		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
107		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
108		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
109		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
110		// compute wasted/effective spend.
111		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
112		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
113		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
114		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
115		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
116		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
117		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
118		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
119		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
120		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"testing"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
11	)
12	
13	// TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen covers ddx-738edf47
14	// AC #3: on any reviewer terminal failure — nonzero exit, empty output, or
15	// unparseable output — the loop must record a failure event and leave the
16	// bead un-closed so a later attempt can retry. Closing on reviewer failure
17	// is the silent-false-closure surface that 738edf47 eliminates.
18	func TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen(t *testing.T) {
19		tests := []struct {
20			name     string
21			reviewer BeadReviewerFunc
22		}{
23			{
24				name: "reviewer exits non-zero (returns error)",
25				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
26					return nil, errors.New("reviewer harness exited with code 1")
27				}),
28			},
29			{
30				name: "reviewer output empty — parse returns unparseable",
31				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
32					return &ReviewResult{
33						Verdict:   "",
34						RawOutput: "",
35					}, nil
36				}),
37			},
38			{
39				name: "reviewer output unparseable — no recognizable verdict line",
40				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
41					return &ReviewResult{
42						Verdict:   "",
43						RawOutput: "Reviewer crashed mid-stream with no structured verdict.",
44					}, nil
45				}),
46			},
47		}
48		for _, tc := range tests {
49			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
50				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
51				worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
52					Store: store,
53					Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
54						return ExecuteBeadReport{
55							BeadID:    beadID,
56							Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
57							SessionID: "sess-x",
58							ResultRev: "c0ffee" + tc.name[:4],
59						}, nil
60					}),
61					Reviewer: tc.reviewer,
62				}
63	
64				_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
65				require.NoError(t, err)
66	
67				got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
68				require.NoError(t, err)
69				assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
70					"reviewer failure of any kind must not close the bead — the whole point of this invariant is that a broken reviewer cannot silently end work")
71			})
72		}
73	}
74

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"strings"
8		"testing"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// TestReviewErrorTaxonomy covers FEAT-022 §12 / Stage G AC #1: each of the
17	// four review-error classes is emitted distinctly when its root cause fires,
18	// and the appended review-error event body carries the literal class
19	// identifier so operators can triage without re-parsing reviewer text.
20	func TestReviewErrorTaxonomy(t *testing.T) {
21		tests := []struct {
22			name      string
23			wantClass string
24			reviewer  BeadReviewerFunc
25		}{
26			{
27				name:      "context_overflow",
28				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
29				// Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit returns ReviewResult.Error
30				// = OutcomeReviewContextOverflow alongside a wrapped error. The
31				// loop classifier reads the structured Error first.
32				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
33					return &ReviewResult{
34							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
35							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
36							ResultRev: resultRev,
37						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt 200000 bytes exceeds cap 100000)",
38							evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow)
39				}),
40			},
41			{
42				name:      "provider_empty",
43				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
44				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
45					return &ReviewResult{
46							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
47							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
48							ResultRev: resultRev,
49						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
50							ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
51				}),
52			},
53			{
54				name:      "unparseable",
55				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
56				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
57					return &ReviewResult{
58							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
59							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
60							ResultRev: resultRev,
61							RawOutput: "Reviewer text without a structured verdict line.",
62						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
63							ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
64				}),
65			},
66			{
67				name:      "transport",
68				wantClass: evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
69				reviewer: BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
70					return &ReviewResult{
71							Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
72							Error:     evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
73							ResultRev: resultRev,
74						}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
75							errors.New("dial tcp: connection refused"))
76				}),
77			},
78		}
79	
80		for _, tc := range tests {
81			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
82				store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
83				worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
84					Store: store,
85					Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
86						return ExecuteBeadReport{
87							BeadID:    beadID,
88							Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
89							SessionID: "sess-tax-" + tc.name,
90							ResultRev: "rev-" + tc.name,
91						}, nil
92					}),
93					Reviewer: tc.reviewer,
94				}
95	
96				_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
97				require.NoError(t, err)
98	
99				events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
100				require.NoError(t, err)
101				var got *bead.BeadEvent
102				for i := range events {
103					if events[i].Kind == "review-error" {
104						got = &events[i]
105						break
106					}
107				}
108				require.NotNil(t, got, "expected a review-error event for class %s", tc.wantClass)
109				// AC #1: event body must contain the literal class identifier.
110				assert.Contains(t, got.Body, tc.wantClass,
111					"review-error event body must carry the literal class string for operator triage")
112				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantClass, got.Summary,
113					"review-error event summary should be the class identifier")
114				// And the result_rev association must be recorded so the retry
115				// counter can be scoped to the originating commit.
116				assert.True(t, strings.Contains(got.Body, "result_rev=rev-"+tc.name),
117					"event body must include result_rev= for retry-counter scoping")
118			})
119		}
120	}
121
